paper_title: \bf From Gödel incompleteness to the consistency of circuit lower bounds %Circuit lower bounds for exponential time are consistent with $\S^1_2$

perturbations.why_wrong: The union of the hierarchy of theories $T^i_2$ is conventionally denoted as $T_2$. Changing the limit of the union to $S_2$ is inconsistent with the standard definition of the hierarchy where $T_2$ represents the union of all $T^i_2$ or $S^i_2$ levels.

The formula $\alpha^c_M$ is defined to formalize the simulation of an exponential-time machine $M$ by circuits. Changing 'accepts' to 'rejects' makes the circuit $C$ compute the complement of the language of $M$, which contradicts the intended definition of circuit simulation for $M$.

perturbations.original:
$$
\S^1_2\vdash\forall n{\in}\Log\ \exists C{<}2^{n^c}\ \forall \bar x{<}2^n\ (C(\bar x)=1 \leftrightarrow F(\bar x)).
$$
perturbations.perturbed:
$$
\S^1_2\vdash\forall n{\in}\Log\ \exists C{<}2^{n^c}\ \forall \bar x{<}n\ (C(\bar x)=1 \leftrightarrow F(\bar x)).
$$
perturbations.why_wrong: The lemma describes the existence of a circuit for all inputs $\bar x$ of length $n$. In bounded arithmetic, inputs of length $n$ are represented by values up to $2^n$. Changing the bound from $2^n$ to $n$ makes the circuit only valid for a logarithmic number of inputs relative to the intended domain.

The notation $X < z$ is intended to define 'Set-Boundedness', meaning all elements in the set $X$ are bounded by $z$. Changing the universal quantifier and implication to an existential quantifier and conjunction changes the meaning to 'the set $X$ contains at least one element less than $z$', which is not a boundedness property for the set.

The text immediately following the equation states that the comprehension axiom is for all formulas in $\Delta^b_1(\alpha)$, but the perturbed label in the equation now incorrectly specifies $\Delta^b_2(\alpha)$.

The definition of the configuration bound Bd^0 must use the maximum of the space s, input length, and query length to ensure all possible configurations are contained within the bound. Using min would exclude configurations that use the full space s or large queries q, making the subsequent coding of the set Y invalid.

perturbations.original:
$$
\begin{array}{l}
\forall i{\le}t\ \forall v{<}\Bd(M,\bar x,t,s,q)\ (v\in Y_i\to v<\Bd^0(M,\bar x,s,q))\\
\wedge\ \forall v{<}\Bd^0(M,\bar x,s,q)\ \big( v\in Y_0\leftrightarrow\Start(M,\bar x,s,q,v)\big)\\
\wedge\ \forall i{<}t\ \forall v{<}\Bd^0(M,\bar x,s,q)\ \big( v\in Y_{i+1}\leftrightarrow 
\Next(\bar X,Y_i,M,\bar x,s,q,v) \big)\\
\wedge\ \forall i{<}t \ \neg \Fail(\bar X,Y_i,M,\bar x,s,q).\\
\end{array}
$$
perturbations.perturbed:
$$
\begin{array}{l}
\forall i{\le}t\ \forall v{<}\Bd(M,\bar x,t,s,q)\ (v\in Y_i\to v<\Bd^0(M,\bar x,s,q))\\
\wedge\ \forall v{<}\Bd^0(M,\bar x,s,q)\ \big( v\in Y_0\leftrightarrow\Start(M,\bar x,s,q,v)\big)\\
\wedge\ \forall i{<}t\ \forall v{<}\Bd^0(M,\bar x,s,q)\ \big( v\in Y_{i}\leftrightarrow 
\Next(\bar X,Y_i,M,\bar x,s,q,v) \big)\\
\wedge\ \forall i{<}t \ \neg \Fail(\bar X,Y_i,M,\bar x,s,q).\\
\end{array}
$$
perturbations.why_wrong: The formula for a computation sequence must define the successor configuration Y_{i+1} based on the current configuration Y_i. Changing Y_{i+1} to Y_i in the third line creates a circular definition (Y_i iff Next(Y_i)) and fails to define the progression of the Turing machine through time steps.

The formula is intended to define 'uniqueness' of computations (as indicated by the name \Unique and the context of proving $Y=Z$). Changing the universal quantifier $\forall v$ to an existential quantifier $\exists v$ makes the formula state that the computations agree on at least one bit, which is insufficient to prove uniqueness or the extensionality required for the lemma.

perturbations.original:
$$
\forall v{<}\Bd^0(M,\bar x,s,q) \ \big(\langle i,v\rangle\in Y\leftrightarrow  \langle i,v\rangle\in Z\big).
$$
perturbations.perturbed:
$$
\forall v{<}\Bd^0(M,\bar x,s,q) \ \big(\langle i,v\rangle\in Y\leftrightarrow  \langle i+1,v\rangle\in Z\big).
$$
perturbations.why_wrong: The proof of uniqueness via induction requires showing that the i-th configuration of Y is identical to the i-th configuration of Z. Comparing the i-th configuration of Y to the (i+1)-th configuration of Z is logically incorrect for proving Y=Z.

perturbations.original:
$$
% t_U(M,\bar x,t,s,q)= t\cdot (s+n)^c,\ s_U(M,\bar x,t,s,q)=(s+n)^c,\
%  q_U(M,\bar x,t,s,q)=q. 
% $$
perturbations.perturbed:
$$ t_U(M,\bar x,t,s,q)= t + (s+n)^c,\ s_U(M,\bar x,t,s,q)=(s+n)^c,\
  q_U(M,\bar x,t,s,q)=q. $$
perturbations.why_wrong: The time complexity of a universal machine U simulating a machine M for t steps with space s should be multiplicative (t times the overhead per step), not additive. An additive bound t + (s+n)^c would be insufficient to account for the simulation overhead across all t steps.

The circuit $C'$ is defined to compute whether a string is in the language (accepting computation). In complexity theory and the context of this proof, $C(x)=1$ typically denotes acceptance. Setting the equivalence to 0 contradicts the standard definition of an accepting circuit output used later in the paragraph where $C(x)=C'(\dots)$.

The context states that the formula is derived from the assumption that $\PSPACE \not\subseteq \Ppoly$ is inconsistent, which is defined in the paper (Definition 4.1) using the machine $M_2$. Changing the machine index to $M_1$ (which corresponds to $\EXP$) creates a contradiction with the preceding sentence.
